report_name: Microsoft Details 'GigaWiper,' a Destructive Multi-Tool Windows Backdoor Linked to Iranian APT

threat_actors.name: Iranian-backed Threat Actor
threat_actors.description: An Iranian-backed advanced persistent threat (APT) group believed to be responsible for developing and deploying the GigaWiper malware for sabotage and destructive purposes.
threat_actors.goals: Sabotage and data destruction for political or strategic purposes.

attack_patterns.name: Disk Wipe
attack_patterns.description: GigaWiper implements this via two modules: one for a full disk wipe across all connected drives, and another for overwriting the Windows operating system drive.

attack_patterns.name: Disk Content Wipe
attack_patterns.description: GigaWiper contains a module to systematically overwrite data across all connected drives, rendering it unrecoverable.

attack_patterns.name: Disk Structure Wipe
attack_patterns.description: GigaWiper has a function to overwrite the critical files of the Windows operating system, effectively bricking the OS.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The 'fake ransomware' module mimics ransomware by encrypting files to cause disruption and mislead incident responders.

attack_patterns.name: Data Destruction
attack_patterns.description: The 'fake ransomware' module's true purpose is data destruction, as it generates and immediately discards the encryption key, making recovery impossible.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: The malware likely deletes volume shadow copies using tools like vssadmin before wiping data to prevent easy restoration of files.

malware.name: GigaWiper
malware.description: A modular Windows backdoor with three destructive functions: full disk wipe, OS drive overwrite, and a 'fake ransomware' module that irreversibly scrambles files. It is designed for sabotage and data destruction.

tools.name: vssadmin
tools.description: Used by attackers to delete volume shadow copies to inhibit system recovery before wiping data.

indicators.name: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
id: 2b9a8c7d-1e3f-4b5a-9d6c-8a7b6c5d4e3f
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common precursor to ransomware or wiper attacks.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-10
tags:
  - attack.t1490
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\vssadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'delete'
      - 'shadows'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common precursor to ransomware or wiper attacks like GigaWiper.
